claim: Between 1985 and 1986, the Reagan administration secretly sold anti-tank and anti-aircraft missiles to the Islamic Republic of Iran — with which the United States had no diplomatic relations and which the State Department had designated a state sponsor of terrorism — in exchange for Iranian help releasing American hostages held by Hezbollah in Lebanon. Profits from the arms sales were diverted to fund the Contras, anti-Sandinista paramilitaries in Nicaragua whom Congress had explicitly barred from receiving U.S. assistance under the Boland Amendments. The scheme was run out of the National Security Council by Lt. Col. Oliver North and National Security Adviser John Poindexter, with logistical assistance routed through Israel and through arms dealer Adnan Khashoggi (an introducer to Jeffrey Epstein in some accounts via Robert Maxwell's circle). Independent Counsel Lawrence E. Walsh's August 1993 final report concluded that senior Reagan-administration officials, including the President, knew about and engaged in efforts to conceal the operations. Eleven officials, including Cabinet member Caspar Weinberger (Secretary of Defense), were convicted or pleaded guilty; six were pardoned by President George H. W. Bush in December 1992.

sources.label: Lawrence E. Walsh, Final Report of the Independent Counsel for Iran/Contra Matters, Volume I — Investigations and Prosecutions (August 4, 1993).
U.S. Congress — Report of the Congressional Committees Investigating the Iran-Contra Affair (House Select Committee and Senate Select Committee), November 18, 1987.
National Security Archive (GWU) — Iran-Contra Affair document collection.
Theodore Draper, A Very Thin Line: The Iran-Contra Affairs (Hill and Wang, 1991).

notes: The Walsh Independent Counsel report is the canonical primary record. Draper's 1991 book is the most thorough narrative secondary. The reel's mention of Khashoggi's role is accurate — he was one of several private intermediaries in the arms-sales channel; his connection to Robert Maxwell and the broader Epstein-adjacent social circle is documented in subsequent reporting but is downstream of the canonical Iran-Contra record.
